title: Pneumatopteris parksii
taxonomy: Pneumatopteris parksii belongs to the family Dryniaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the air plants or resurrection ferns. This classification is due to their unique ability to survive for extended periods without water by drying out and rehydrating when moisture becomes available. The genus Pneumatopteris is characterized by its unique sporophyte structure and the presence of pneumatophores, which are specialized aerial roots that facilitate gas exchange. Pneumatopteris parksii is a species of epiphytic fern native to Southeast Asia, where it is often found growing on tree trunks and branches. The classification of Pneumatopteris parksii places it within the division Pteridophyta, a group of vascular plants that also includes other ferns and lycopods.
